claim: Windsurf's 'flow state' design keeps you in a single integrated experience with the AI agent—avoiding the disrupt of switching between IDE and separate AI tools
actionable: Prefer IDE-integrated coding agents that show you changes in real-time; avoid context switching between separate tools
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help

claim: When coding agent conversations get long, restart the conversation and re-explain your context instead of letting hallucinations compound—this is faster than iterating through errors
actionable: Monitor conversation length; restart and re-explain rather than adding more fixes to a long conversation
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help

claim: Give one focused request at a time to coding agents—multiple requirements in a single prompt cause confusion and hallucinations even with chain-of-thought reasoning
actionable: Break multi-part changes into sequential requests; wait for the agent to correct each change before moving to the next
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help

claim: Test changes before accepting them in your IDE—the agent actually implements pending changes, so test in the app first, then reject unwanted changes without manual revert
actionable: After the agent proposes changes, run your app and test; use reject/accept to selectively apply only working changes
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help

claim: Use @ mentions to reference specific files or functions when directing the agent—this prevents hallucinations and ensures it edits the right code
actionable: When asking for changes, use @file or @function syntax to explicitly reference the code you want modified
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help

claim: Define your tech stack explicitly and early (e.g., 'use FastAPI + Python + Postgres' instead of 'build me an API')—specific tech stacks prevent the agent from guessing incorrectly
actionable: At the start of any project, explicitly specify all technologies, frameworks, and database choices; be specific about schema and API design
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help

claim: When a coding agent gets stuck, switch to Claude 3.5 Sonnet or O1 for targeted debugging—paste your code and error into the better model, get the fix, apply it, then resume with the original agent
actionable: Use Claude 3.5 Sonnet or O1 as a debug partner when Windsurf hallucinations occur; copy fixes back into your codebase
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help

claim: Use the refactor/explain buttons on specific functions to edit/understand targeted code—focused context reduces hallucinations vs. asking the agent to search the entire codebase
actionable: When making changes, select the function/class in the IDE and use refactor/explain instead of describing changes in chat
source_title: I'm Addicted to Windsurf, Send Help
